page: Amazon Web Services m8id.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The system is equipped with 512 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4 GB of RAM per core.

For local storage, the instance includes 7600 GB of bundled NVMe SSD instance store capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a 50 Gbps baseline bandwidth. The combination of high-speed DDR5 memory and direct-attached NVMe storage makes this instance suitable for data-intensive operations without relying solely on network-attached storage.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached transfers exceeding 10,000 GB/sec and large block uncached reads reaching 392.68 GB/sec. Database operations, including Redis SET and Passmark database tests, also rank in the top 10%, alongside strong performance in LLM inference prompt processing across small, medium, and large models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8id.32xlarge offers high resource density by combining high-frequency compute, massive DDR5 memory bandwidth, and substantial local NVMe storage within a single general-purpose profile. This eliminates the need for separate high-performance storage attachments for temporary data. Appropriate workloads include large-scale relational databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 128 vCPUs, 64 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed. Built on the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture, it features 512 GB of DDR5 RAM and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers top-tier performance, with benchmark results placing its multi-core CPU capabilities, memory bandwidth, and database operations in the top 10% of tested systems. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in LLM inference prompt processing. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-speed local storage, the m8id.32xlarge is highly efficient for resource-dense deployments. It is optimized for demanding workloads such as large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, Redis caching layers, and CPU-based machine learning tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.32xlarge features 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage for high-performance database and compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density general-purpose instance combining 128 vCPUs, DDR5 memory, and 7.6 TB of local NVMe storage for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 128 vCPUs and Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz
Equipped with 512 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Includes 7600 GB of direct-attached local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Well-suited for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and LLM inference
